description: Validate brittle or destructible rigid-body fracture against an explicitly declared damage-response model rather than a glass-specific visual template.
physical_assumptions: A brittle body declares one damage-response model, its threshold units, and a minimum fragment evidence requirement.
The impact_energy model compares runtime impact energy in joules with fracture_threshold_j.

The contact_external_strain model applies declared Chaos external strain only after runtime contact and compares it with the Geometry Collection damage threshold; strain must never be relabeled as joules.

When minimum_impact_energy_j is declared, the Harness measures translational kinetic energy from the UE impactor component's last pre-contact sample and must not issue the strain command below that gate.

energy_response_levels may declare a strictly increasing measured-energy curve; runtime selects the highest reached level and records its damage_state, strain and optional radial impulse rather than choosing a preset from the requested label.
A positive contact_external_strain result requires native collision provenance and the runtime Geometry Collection threshold; bounds inference and CaseSpec threshold echo are diagnostic only.

When center_source is native_contact_impact_point, the external-strain command and fracture event must use the native UE hit point, and the verifier compares both centers within the declared centimeter tolerance.

A radial prefracture pattern can be authored around a planned impact region, but Chaos strain does not rebuild Geometry Collection topology at runtime. Arbitrary emergent hit locations require a low-cost contact probe, a cached impact-centered asset build, and a deterministic rerun from the same initial state.

Validated causal axes are incident energy and planned impact position. Each matrix changes one axis at a time; a position condition binds target point, launch position, and the matching impact-centered Geometry Collection while preserving energy, map, camera, and timebase.
Below-energy-gate impacts are valid intact controls: contact must be recorded, the strain command must be absent, and no fracture event may appear.
Fragments are runtime evidence and must have explicit stable IDs, source ownership, per-shard state, and a cross-pass state hash; rendered debris alone is insufficient.
required_assets: fracture fragment mesh or analytic fragment proxy
verifier_rules: Brittle object declares either positive fracture_threshold_j for impact_energy or positive external_strain plus damage_thresholds for contact_external_strain.
Expected impactor-brittle contact pair appears in contact events.
The runtime response value is present, uses the declared model and units, and reaches the runtime-reported threshold when fracture occurs.

If minimum_impact_energy_j is configured, impact_energy_j, a strictly earlier energy_sample_frame, energy_model, the gate decision, and external_strain_applied are recorded on the causal contact; a missing pre-contact sample fails closed, strain below the gate or fracture below the gate is a causality violation.

If energy_response_levels is configured, the recorded damage_state and external strain must match the level selected from measured pre-contact energy; radial_impulse_applied is true only after the UE component accepts the impulse call.
When center_source=native_contact_impact_point, fracture_center_source must name that source and fracture_center_cm must match the causal native contact impact_point_cm within tolerance.

An impact-position matrix keeps energy and acquisition fixed, uniquely labels every condition, and keeps target point, impactor launch X, planned fracture-center X, and cached Geometry Collection selection consistent.
If expected_fracture is declared, the observed intact/fractured outcome must match it in both solver truth and RGB/data sensor passes.
contact_external_strain requires a native collision event and rejects bounds-inferred contact as final evidence.
A positive fracture requires a native Chaos break-event marker; polling root-broken state alone remains diagnostic.
Fracture frame is not earlier than the causal contact frame.
Geometry Collection root-broken state is true for contact_external_strain fracture.
Fragment count and explicit stable fragment lineage are runtime artifacts and satisfy expected_min_fragment_count.
RGB and data passes share matching fracture event keys and explicit fragment-state hashes.
repair_suggestions: Declare impact_energy or contact_external_strain explicitly, including threshold units and expected_min_fragment_count.
Export impact energy for the impact_energy model, or applied external strain plus the runtime Geometry Collection damage thresholds for the Chaos strain model.
Replace bounds contact inference with native Chaos collision provenance before promoting a diagnostic.
Move any pre-contact break animation into a post-contact fracture event.
Export fragment count and stable fragment lineage; a visible break without those artifacts remains diagnostic only.
Do not describe a fixed or planned radial Voronoi asset as arbitrary runtime crack generation; use probe-build-rerun when the impact location is not known during scene construction.
Use fit_dynamic_plan=false for airborne impactors or suspended brittle bodies whose declared height must not be snapped to a support surface.
